definition: The 2026–2028 maturity wall colliding with demographic-driven labor scarcity creates a dual squeeze on leveraged borrowers from both refinancing costs (higher rates) and operating costs (labor inflation), likely pushing HY defaults above consensus.
evidence: $1.5–3T in maturities depending on scope definition
Refinancing at SOFR+450–600 vs. original SOFR+300–400
Modeled HY default rate reaching 5.2–5.8% vs. consensus ~3.5% in moderate recession
Shrinking peak-spending cohort (35–54) reduces revenue runway for consumer-facing borrowers
open_questions: How many borrowers have already refinanced
